maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday August 30, 2018 at 20:30

did.unittitle: Jozef Pilsudski Institute The Belvedere Archives

originalsloc.head: Existence and Location of Originals
originalsloc.p: The entire collection can be located in the Jozef Piłsudski Institute of America Archives (MS 739). The papers consist of holdings from the Jozef Piłsudski Institute of America for Research in the Modern History of Poland. Included are selected papers (1910-1971) which contain the archives of the Polish embassy in London (1912-1937) and of the Polish consulate in New York (1930-1945). The records (Microfilm 887, 774 reels) contain extensive transcripts of evidence from Polish trial records and records of Polish-American organizations. Also included are the private papers of important figures in Polish political life, among them Józef Beck, Józef Lipski and Józef Piłsudski. The so-called Belvedere Archives (Microfilm 550, 31 reels) contain secret documents (November 1918-December 1922) of the Military Chancellery of the Commander-in-Chief of the Polish Army, i.e. Marshal Piłsudski. These papers cover military matters, foreign relations, and internal affairs. Also included are the files of General Tadeusz Rozwadowski, Chief of the General Staff of the Polish Army, for the year 1920.

Written authorization from the Jozef Piłsudski Institute of America for Research in the Modern History of Poland, 381 Park Avenue South, New York, NY 10016 is required in order to copy material on Film MISC 550 or Film MISC 887.
Originals are at the Jozef Piłsudski Institute of America for Research in the Modern History of Poland.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Patrons must obtain permission to copy material. Written authorization from the Jozef Piłsudski Institute of America for Research in the Modern History of Poland, 381 Park Avenue South, New York, NY 10016 is required in order to copy material on Film MISC 550 or Film MISC 887.
